content.full_text:
April 11, 1990

John Rothmer, Attorney
State Public Practise Commission
Legal Division

Re: Our Telephone Conversation of April 3, 1990.

John, as requested here are the specifics
asked about and would appreciate
a written opinion.

My name is Tom Sievres residing at
2607 W. 15th Street, Redondo Beach, California.
My question is "Can one person hold two
elective offices concurrently?"

The specifics are: I was first appointed
to fill a one year term as a Trustee on
the South Bay Union High School District
Board. Through the election process,
I was voted in for a Two year term
1987 1989, and a Four year term
from 1989 to 1993. The School district
covers the entire city boundaries of Redondo
Beach, Hermosa Beach and Manhattan Beach.

In March 1991 there will be an election
in District 3 in Redondo Beach for
Councilman. The 5 CityDistrict covers
approximately, on fifths of the city of
Redondo Beach.

Page Two

If I decide to seek the Coordinatorship
Seat in District Five and be elected,
would I be required to resign my
position on the South Bay Union
High School Board?

I would appreciate hearing from
you on the matter at your earliest
Convenience.

Sincerely,

Tom Dunn
2607 W 159th St
Redondo Beach, Ca 90278

sections.question: Can one person hold two elective offices concurrently? If I decide to seek the Councilman seat in District Five and be elected, would I be required to resign my position on the South Bay Union High School Board.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Tom Downs can simultaneously hold the office of Trustee on the South Bay Union High School District Board and serve as Councilman for District 5 in Redondo Beach, or whether holding the City Council seat would require resignation from the School Board position.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a constituent to FPPC attorney John Rothman, not an FPPC response letter. No conclusion or advice section present. The document clearly identifies the legal question regarding concurrent office holding. No section headers found but content structure is clear.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can one person hold two elective offices concurrently? If I decide to seek the Councilman seat in District Five and be elected, would I be required to resign my position on the South Bay Union High School Board.

John, as requested here are the specifics asked about and would appreciate a written opinion. My name is Tom Sievres residing at 2607 W. 15th Street, Redondo Beach, California. My question is "Can one person hold two elective offices concurrently?" The specifics are: I was first appointed to fill a one year term as a Trustee on the South Bay Union High School District Board. Through the election process, I was voted in for a Two year term 1987 1989, and a Four year term from 1989 to 1993. The School district covers the entire city boundaries of Redondo Beach, Hermosa Beach and Manhattan Beach. In March 1991 there will be an election in District 3 in Redondo Beach for Councilman. The 5 CityDistrict covers approximately, on fifths of the city of Redondo Beach. Page Two If I decide to seek the Coordinatorship Seat in District Five and be elected, would I be required to resign my position on the South Bay Union High School Board? I would appreciate hearing from you on the matter at your earliest Convenience. Sincerely, Tom Dunn 2607 W 159th St Redondo Beach, Ca 90278
embedding.summary: Tom Downs seeks guidance on whether he can hold two elective offices concurrently—specifically, whether he could serve as a School Board Trustee while also serving as a City Councilman for District 5 in Redondo Beach.
